Acceptance Notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

Title: Change management
Requirement: The implementation shall maintain stakeholder analysis, communications, adoption measures, feedback channels, resistance management, champion networks, and leadership reinforcement.
Acceptance Notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

Title: Operational readiness
Requirement: Production release shall require approved service ownership, support model, runbooks, monitoring, continuity, security, data stewardship, release management, and known-issue disposition.
Acceptance Notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

Title: Benefits measurement
Requirement: The program shall baseline and measure cycle time, data quality, reporting effort, portfolio visibility, resource forecast accuracy, decision latency, delivery performance, and user adoption after rollout.
Acceptance Notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

Title: ServiceNow fit-gap validation
Requirement: The implementation team shall validate every requirement against the licensed ServiceNow release and modules, classify it as out-of-box, configure, extend, integrate, process, or deferred, and obtain product-owner approval before build.
Acceptance Notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

Title: Customization guardrail
Requirement: Custom development shall require documented business value, alternatives considered, lifecycle owner, security review, automated tests, upgrade impact, and retirement plan.
Acceptance Notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

Title: Acceptance and traceability
Requirement: Each release shall trace approved requirements to design, configuration or code, test cases, defects, user acceptance, training, and production verification.
Acceptance Notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

Title: Continuous improvement
Requirement: The product team shall maintain a governed backlog, quarterly value review, user feedback process, performance analytics, and roadmap for iterative improvement.
Acceptance Notes: MVP reference implementation; see known limitations and acceptance checklist.

Title: Division visual identity
Requirement: The application shall display a responsive, accessible, division-specific banner on division portfolio views without layout shift and with reduced-motion support.
Acceptance Notes: Six optimized WebP banners render with reserved aspect ratio, accessible alternative text, responsive crop, fade-in, fallback, and reduced-motion behavior.

Title: Canonical division briefing view
Requirement: The division portfolio page shall provide current and briefing presentation modes from the same source-backed view.
Acceptance Notes: Current view, briefing view, presentation mode, and review workspace links are available from the canonical division page.

Title: Governed division profile
Requirement: Authorized division stewards shall maintain mission, vision, focus areas, responsibilities, branches, initiatives, relationships, forums, doctrine, banner metadata, source documents, and review status.
Acceptance Notes: Division profiles are stored in a governed table, editable by approved roles, sourced, date-stamped, and audit recorded.

Title: Authoritative division naming
Requirement: The application shall use Coalition Interoperability Division for CID while preserving the stable CID code, database identifier, records, and links.
Acceptance Notes: CID, JFID, and C3OD2 display names are corrected through an idempotent migration and seed reconciliation without changing stable organization codes or IDs.

Title: Division JSON and CSV export
Requirement: Users shall export accessible division profile and portfolio data as a versioned JSON package or a multi-file CSV package.
Acceptance Notes: JSON and ZIP-contained CSV exports include profile, projects, demands, core functions, capacity, financials, milestones, and RAID while honoring accessible sensitivity scope.

Title: Division profile import preview
Requirement: Authorized profile stewards shall import division profile JSON or CSV only after a non-destructive validation and preview step.
Acceptance Notes: JSON and CSV profile imports are size-limited, parsed, normalized, previewed, validated, explicitly committed, and audit recorded.

Title: Controlled travel-request import
Requirement: The application shall validate, preview, and commit traveler-level approval-source records while retaining source filename, row, external identifier, raw payload, and import batch evidence.
Acceptance Notes: The supplied approval export imports as 385 records totaling $1,082,395.25; one source date anomaly is retained as a warning.

Title: Controlled trip-report import
Requirement: The application shall preserve complete post-trip narratives, detect sensitivity markings, preview validation results, and create traceable trip-report records.
Acceptance Notes: Nine supplied trip reports import with purpose, discussion, findings, recommendations, and action-item narratives intact.

Title: Trip report matching and human reconciliation
Requirement: The application shall score candidate travel-request matches using traveler, division, dates, event title, and location, auto-link only high-confidence unique matches, and require a steward for ambiguous matches.
Acceptance Notes: Match status, confidence, rationale, confirmation actor, and confirmation timestamp are retained.

Title: Travel dashboard and drill-through
Requirement: Authorized users shall filter enterprise travel estimates and drill from dashboard metrics to requests, engagements, reports, and source evidence.
Acceptance Notes: Dashboard includes status, division, month, location, report gaps, reconciliation, and linked detail views.

Title: Engagement-level consolidation
Requirement: The application shall consolidate related traveler requests and trip reports under a reusable engagement while retaining traveler-level cost and approval records.
Acceptance Notes: Canonical engagements aggregate participating divisions, traveler requests, reports, estimated cost, and cross-division status.

Title: Promote trip-report outcomes
Requirement: Authorized reviewers shall classify report findings, recommendations, actions, risks, decisions, and dependencies and promote accepted items to canonical portfolio records with backlinks.
Acceptance Notes: Structured report items preserve their exact source report and promotion state; original narratives remain immutable evidence.

Title: Division travel integration
Requirement: Division portfolio pages shall show travel estimates, recent requests, trip-report outcomes, missing reports, and reconciliation workload for that division.
Acceptance Notes: Division pages link directly to the filtered travel workspace and underlying records.

Title: Travel briefing snapshot integration
Requirement: Division briefings shall include a source-backed travel and external-engagement section whose approved snapshots remain immutable.
Acceptance Notes: The section includes request count, estimated cost, report count, review workload, reconciliation, and linked source records.

Title: Travel provenance and audit evidence
Requirement: Every travel request, report, outcome, match, review, and promoted link shall expose stable identifiers and retain source and audit evidence.
Acceptance Notes: Source filename, row, source record, batch, raw payload, timestamps, match evidence, and audit events are retained.

Title: Travel role and sensitivity enforcement
Requirement: Travel requests, trip reports, and outcomes shall honor organization scope, role authority, and independent sensitivity controls.
Acceptance Notes: Restricted records are excluded for users without sensitive access; write functions require portfolio-management roles.

Title: Travel data-quality controls
Requirement: The application shall surface invalid date sequences, overdue missing reports, and unmatched trip reports as governed data-quality issues.
Acceptance Notes: Source anomalies are not silently corrected; they are retained and assigned for steward disposition.

Title: Travel CSV export and linked portability
Requirement: Authorized users shall export accessible travel requests and trip reports as machine-readable CSV and include both datasets in division export packages.
Acceptance Notes: Exports include stable IDs, source identifiers, engagement links, dates, estimates, report status, and provenance fields.

Title: Simplified Briefings navigation
Requirement: The application shall label the leadership briefing workspace as Briefings while retaining stable portfolio-review routes, records, permissions, and audit evidence.
Acceptance Notes: User-facing labels are simplified without a route or data migration.

Title: Interactive geographic footprint
Requirement: Authorized users shall view accessible travel activity on an interactive geographic footprint with proportional cost markers and linked request, engagement, division, and report counts.
Acceptance Notes: Map markers are generated only from already-authorized server-side aggregates.

Title: Governed location normalization
Requirement: The application shall normalize known destination aliases and typographical variants to stewarded canonical locations while surfacing unmapped values and mapping coverage.
Acceptance Notes: Canonical city-level reference coordinates are packaged locally; source location text remains unchanged on records.

Title: Map and Top Locations cross-filtering
Requirement: The geographic map and Top Locations index shall coordinate selection and link back to a stable location-filtered travel workspace.
Acceptance Notes: Location links preserve authorized server-side filtering and record drill-through.

Title: Travel trend and determination analytics
Requirement: The travel workspace shall visualize monthly estimated cost and request volume plus determination mix by division with accessible labels and linked filters.
Acceptance Notes: Charts use the same filtered request population as the tables and KPIs.

Title: Travel outcome funnel and report compliance
Requirement: The travel workspace shall trace approved travel through completion, linked report, review, and promoted outcome stages and identify division report gaps.
Acceptance Notes: Funnel and compliance counts reconcile to accessible requests, reports, and promoted report items.

Title: Portfolio Investment Flow Sankey
Requirement: The Portfolio Overview shall present approved investment as a flow through financial category, lead division, project, and actual-to-date or unspent-approved outcomes.
Acceptance Notes: The visualization is labeled Investment Flow because the local baseline is not an authoritative cash ledger.

Title: Reconciled flow and drill-through
Requirement: The investment-flow visualization shall conserve approved budget, expose its basis and reconciliation state, and provide drill-through to contributing financial and project records.
Acceptance Notes: Approved equals actual-to-date capped to approved plus unspent approved; forecast variance remains a separate metric.

Title: Accessible alternatives for advanced visuals
Requirement: Interactive map and flow visualizations shall support keyboard selection, descriptive labels, reduced dependence on color, and table or linked-list alternatives.
Acceptance Notes: Core navigation and source access remain available when JavaScript is unavailable.

Title: Local visualization assets and scoped aggregates
Requirement: Travel map and portfolio flow visualizations shall use local assets and shall not send destination, financial, or user data to external map, chart, geocoding, or CDN services.
Acceptance Notes: The existing self-only CSP remains compatible with the release.

Title: Simplified primary navigation
Requirement: The application shall present approximately nine primary navigation destinations per role, with less-frequent workspaces and administration functions in collapsible groups, without removing any authorized capability.
Acceptance Notes: Group open state persists per user and auto-expands for active routes.

Title: Contextual shortcut strip
Requirement: The top strip shall provide contextual shortcuts (Home, My Work with open count, top role actions, Notifications) instead of duplicating the sidebar hierarchy.
Acceptance Notes: Replaces the duplicated primary top tabs.

Title: Unified Action Center
Requirement: My Work shall present one prioritized queue grouped by attention level (Critical now, Awaiting me, Due soon, Needs update, Watching, Recently completed) with per-item rationale, parent, priority, due date, status, and one primary action; empty groups shall collapse and an all-clear state shall replace empty panels.
Acceptance Notes: Briefing questions, change requests, travel follow-ups, stale status, and health deterioration feed the same queue.

Title: Governed quick actions
Requirement: Users shall update task percent complete, complete tasks, record blockers, and close actions inline from My Work; quick actions shall enforce CSRF, restrict to the owner or managing project manager, and record audit evidence with before and after values.
Acceptance Notes: Immediate-save pattern only; explicit-submission workflows unchanged.

Title: Decision-first Portfolio Overview
Requirement: The Portfolio Overview shall present Decisions Required and Significant Changes before health and investment content, and shall relocate the Investment Flow diagram to an Investment analysis section.
Acceptance Notes: Changes feed covers health deterioration, critical risks, forecast breach, milestone slippage, and stale status.

Title: Explainable health rollup
Requirement: The Portfolio Health indicator shall provide a View calculation control showing the formula, status counts, effective-health precedence per contributing project, and data freshness.
Acceptance Notes: Distinguishes calculated, owner-reported, and leadership-override health.

Title: Focused Travel workspaces
Requirement: The Travel area shall be decomposed into Overview, Requests, Trip Reports, Reconciliation, and Engagement Outcomes views with server-side pagination limited to 25 rows per page and filter preservation across views.
Acceptance Notes: Initial page loads no longer render hundreds of records.

Title: Adaptive guidance, onboarding, and glossary
Requirement: The application shall compact role guidance for returning users, collapse page guides after first visit, provide a role-specific restartable first-login checklist, and provide a searchable help and glossary drawer with meaning, importance, owner, and authoritative source per term.
Acceptance Notes: Preferences persist per browser; reopen via topbar Help.

Title: Adoption measurement hooks
Requirement: The application shall record privacy-conscious UX adoption events locally with no network calls, no identifiers, and no record content, structured for later connection of approved analytics tooling.
Acceptance Notes: 200-event local ring buffer; drain via window.jsj6Telemetry.drain().

Title: Version single source of truth
Requirement: All release artifacts (application metadata, UI footer, static asset strings, export schemas, package documentation) shall derive from the VERSION file, and stale test expectations shall be root-caused rather than suppressed.
Acceptance Notes: Resolves v0.7.7/v0.7.8 version drift.
